pattern: Sort the array, then use memoised DP over (index, count chosen, last chosen index, current minimum gap) to accumulate the sum of minimum-gap values across all length-k subsequences.
hints: Sort nums first; this ensures the minimum absolute difference in any subsequence equals the minimum consecutive gap among sorted selected elements.

Define dp(i, remaining, prev_idx, min_gap): from index i, choosing `remaining` more elements, where the last chosen was prev_idx and current minimum inter-element gap is min_gap. When remaining==0, add min_gap to the result.
The state space is O(n^2 * k * D) where D is the number of distinct gap values. With n <= 50 and k <= 50 this is feasible. Use a dict for memoisation and work modulo 10^9+7.

edge_inputs.description: all same value, power is always 0
k equals n, only one subsequence
large values with both positive and negative to check absolute difference
duplicate values cause some gaps to be 0, reducing power

stress.description: small arrays with k up to half length to verify DP correctness
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(-10**6, 10**6) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(2, n)
    return [nums, k]
